rationale: The cited paper is used as a methodological source for how to probe and interpret interaction effects, not just as general background. In Studies 2, 3, and 4, the authors explicitly invoke Johnson-Neyman floodlight analysis and cite Spiller et al. (2013) when conducting those conditional-effect tests. This citation plays a concrete role in their empirical analysis workflow, even though it is not central to their theory contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s interaction-probing guidance (especially floodlight/spotlight style simple-effects interpretation) to analyze its moderated regression results. It uses that approach to identify regions where ideology-related effects are significant across studies.
